Smart Contract.why: Audited vault and gateway with limited upgrade surface; standard ERC-4626 implementation.

Protocol.why: Mature underlying protocols and diversified strategies reduce single-protocol blow-up risk.

Chain.why: Base L2 with strong security assumptions and active monitoring.

Liquidity.why: Stablecoin pair depth is sufficient for normal-sized redemptions without heavy slippage.

Strategy.why: Vault capital is only routed into whitelisted strategies with per-strategy caps; operator-managed.

Bridging.why: USD exposure may involve bridge or fiat rails; redemption can be delayed during stress.

Smart Contract.why: Same ERC-4626 standard and audit posture as other yoTokens.

Protocol.why: ETH staking or lending protocols carry slashing or liquidation risk if conditions turn.

Liquidity.why: Large redemptions can move price; in volatile periods a queue may apply.

Strategy.why: Vault capital can be routed into external DeFi strategies that may underperform or become temporarily illiquid.

Bridging.why: Native ETH on Base; no bridge dependency for the asset itself.

Smart Contract.why: Same vault standard and audit as other yoTokens.

Protocol.why: Wrapped BTC and custodial/synthetic protocols add counterparty and depeg risk.

Liquidity.why: WBTC/renBTC liquidity and redemption delays can show up in stressed markets.

Strategy.why: Limited set of strategies for BTC yield; concentration and protocol risk in those venues.

Bridging.why: Asynchronous redemption during high volatility can mean you wait for settlement.

Protocol.why: Stable yield sources with lower headline risk than volatile assets.

Liquidity.why: EUR stablecoin liquidity can be thinner than USD, so larger redemptions may have more impact.

Bridging.why: Fiat rails and cross-border flows can introduce redemption delays.

Smart Contract.why: Same codebase; typically smaller TVL than major yoTokens.

Protocol.why: Commodity-backed or synthetic gold protocols are less battle-tested and can have custody or peg risk.

Liquidity.why: Gold token liquidity is limited; redemptions can be slow or at worse prices in stress.

Strategy.why: Fewer strategies and higher concentration; one underperforming or illiquid strategy has a bigger impact.

Bridging.why: Tokenized gold often involves off-chain or cross-chain settlement and timing risk.
